title: Reopen product UX and playable NPC action blocker
summary: Record Owner post-closeout blocker that the current Web shell still fails product playability: information hierarchy is overwhelming, RimWorld-like player loop is absent, NPC action is not sufficiently player-perceptible, and building/job command loops are missing from the product surface. Produce a reviewed remediation DAG before further implementation.
acceptance: Owner post-closeout blocker is recorded without minimizing the critique: current UI overwhelms information hierarchy, does not feel like a game, does not communicate player goals, and lacks a player-visible RimWorld-like order/work/build loop

Report explicitly corrects the acceptance standard: NPCs must move and act in a way the player can perceive and understand; idle, moving, working, blocked, completed and failed states must be visually legible before any future playable-product readiness claim

Current implementation facts are recorded honestly: Web shell is a static product-gate read-model surface; PlayerCommand currently supports only Noop/Echo; WM-0138 lamp priority is shell-local and non-authoritative; sim-core has job/build modules not yet exposed as a product loop
Art-thread consultation is requested and either incorporated if returned or recorded as pending with the exact thread id and request summary

A non-executed remediation DAG is drafted for the next phase, including product UX hierarchy, perceptible NPC autonomy, authoritative command/job integration, build/place/order loop, renderer motion feedback, first-five-minute guidance, E2E visual/playtest evidence and independent review

No product implementation, public release, EA, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services, public save compatibility, platform verdict change or fake non-authoritative gameplay workaround is executed by this task

history.details: Owner blocker task created after WM-0142 was already done; task packet refined and promoted for project-director claim.
thread=Beacon WM-0145 product UX blocker reopen

Recorded Owner product UX blocker and self-correction; incorporated art-thread feedback; reopened product UX remediation in project-state; created non-executed WM-0146..WM-0156 DAG centered on visible NPC action, authoritative lamp/build command-job flow, information hierarchy repair and visual evidence; handoff, taskctl validate/status and diff check pass; no product implementation or release/platform gate changes.

verified: No blocking findings; Owner critique and visible NPC-action product gate are recorded honestly, current shell-local/static facts and art feedback are incorporated, WM-0146..WM-0156 remain proposed as a coherent remediation DAG, release/platform owner gates are preserved, and required control-plane checks pass.
Merged commit e410e7a into main; handoff, taskctl validate/status and git diff --check passed on main; public gates preserved and downstream WM-0146..WM-0156 remain proposed.

forbiddenPaths: telemetry, accounts, hosted feedback or paid services
changing Web demo-only or Windows controlled-test verdicts without owner approval
